candidates.text:
absolutely legendary post-mortem in the works for this one

that one gcp outage due to a flood in france will have nothing on this
there's an AWS outage in me-central-1 because it got bombed
We are facing this problem in our org. How do you migrate your database from Google cloud to AWS with minimal downtime?

We can start through this too,
by setting up continuous replication instead of doing a big-bang dump.

Quick approach for minimal downtime:

• Set up CDC/replication from GCP DB → AWS (DMS/native replication)
• Keep both in sync while traffic still hits GCP
• Backfill historical data first
• Run dual-read validation checks
• Switch traffic via feature flag/DNS cutover
• Keep rollback window ready

Goal: migrate data first, switch traffic last.

Zero-downtime migrations are mostly about sync strategy + safe cutover, not just data copy.

30 lines of bash = ai powered on-call engineer

tail logs → pipe to claude → alert slack

Avg Engineer experience in 2026

> AI ships faster than your sprint
> “Lean team” = you
> Layoffs = “realignment”
> Hiring freeze, workload on fire
> Stock comp = nostalgia
> “We’re family” (until budget cuts)
> On-call = insomnia subscription
> 5 roles. 1 salary.

Senior. Staff. Principal.
Still fixing CSS at 3 AM.
